body.title: Longitudinal psychological state tracking for Igor via NE indicators
body.result: JSONL psych log in coa.py _ne_worker; read_psych_log() utility; valence+arousal+notes per NE run
body.description:
Igor's NE already tracks valence. Extend this to a longitudinal psychological state record that captures valence, uncertainty, cognitive_load, and coherence at each NE run, stored with timestamps in palace or a dedicated DB table. This creates a queryable history — 'how has Igor's valence trended over the last week?' — and feeds into the reasoning inspection audit's interview pass.

Watching Igor's psychological state over time ('as we go along') is the longitudinal health signal that point-in-time inspection can't surface.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/narrative_engine.py, wild_igor/igor/cognition/coa.py, palace table or adc.palace insert, possibly a new igor_psych_log table
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database (Postgres-only); theigors/rules/memory
**Scope boundary:** In scope: capture 4 indicators per NE run, store with timestamp, basic query interface (last N days trend). Out of scope: visualization/dashboard (separate ticket), automated alerting on psych degradation.
**Test plan:** NE run produces psych_state record; records accumulate over time; trend query returns correct time-series; existing NE tests still pass.
